tech_stack.frontend: React 19 + Vite 7.3 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4

codebase_patterns.database_access: SQLite with @contextmanager decorator for resource management. Uses explicit commit/rollback pattern.
codebase_patterns.api_proxy: Vite proxy config routes /api/* to http://localhost:8000 to avoid CORS in dev
codebase_patterns.tailwind_version: Tailwind CSS v4 uses @theme directive with CSS custom properties instead of tailwind.config.js
codebase_patterns.react_patterns: React 19 with StrictMode, functional components with hooks
codebase_patterns.typescript_strict: tsconfig has noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch enabled
codebase_patterns.fastapi_lifespan: Use lifespan context manager pattern (not deprecated @app.on_event)
codebase_patterns.optimistic_updates: Create temp item with temp-{timestamp} ID, replace with real on API success, revert on error
codebase_patterns.toast_notifications: Toast component with auto-dismiss (3s), success/error variants, slide-in animation
codebase_patterns.api_timeout: fetchWithTimeout using AbortController, 10s default, user-friendly error messages

common_mistakes: Do NOT use @app.on_event('startup') - use lifespan context manager instead
Always call load_dotenv() if python-dotenv is installed
CORS origins should be environment-configurable, not hardcoded
Use parameterized queries for all database operations to prevent SQL injection
Define TypeScript interfaces for all API response types
Frontend UpdateItem should NOT send user_id - backend ALLOWED_UPDATE_FIELDS excludes it
Wrap Toast onClose callbacks in useCallback to prevent unnecessary re-renders
architecture_decisions: Health check at /api/health for application health monitoring
Dual health endpoints: / (root info) and /api/health (health check)
Minimal dependencies approach - lean dependency tree
SQLite for development, PostgreSQL for production
